text:
Kurapika's Memories (クラピカ, Kurapika Tsuioku-hen) is a prequel one-shot and "volume zero" of the Hunter × Hunter manga series. It was given out to the first one million viewers of the 2013 feature film, Phantom Rouge.

A digital edition was released by Shueisha on July 4 th , 2023. The Q&A with Togashi and information about the film were not included.

Chapters

1. Kurapika's Memories: Part One (クラピカ①, Kurapika Tsuioku-hen (Ichi))
2. Kurapika's Memories: Part Two (クラピカ②, Kurapika Tsuioku-hen (Ni))

Summary

In the village of the Kurta Clan, a young Kurapika continues to argue with the Elder about not being allowed to go to the outside world. He talks about it with his friend Pairo, who tells him about the rule and how people of the outside world discriminate against their kind. Kurapika then thinks back to a year ago when they met an injured girl named Sheila, who taught them about the outside world and gave them a book called Dino Hunter, which made Kurapika and Pairo want to go on their own adventure. Kurapika eventually convinces the Elder to allow him to take the exit exam.

Kurapika concentrates on studying and easily passes the first two tests. The Elder explains that the final test involves a shopping trip to a neighboring town in the outside world. He then reveals special eyedrops that will cause a Kurta's eyes to stay scarlet if they turn red within 24 hours of administering them. He stresses that Kurapika will fail if his eyes turn scarlet within those 24 hours. After Kurapika chooses Pairo to accompany him, the two take three Piko with them to Nancha City. Kurapika then thinks back on how Pairo saving him from falling off a cliff caused the injuries to his eyes and legs. He vows to find a doctor for him and hopes that they can explore the outside world together with no time limit.

After they reach the town and begin shopping at the department store, three local thugs cause an incident with Pairo. Several bystanders eventually take the side of the two boys, who finish their shopping with their help. However, the three thugs wait for them on the outskirts of town and order them to pay a toll before they can leave. Bystanders again cause the thugs to back down, but after the main thug trips Pairo and calls him a gimp, Kurapika becomes enraged and easily subdues all three, his eyes becoming scarlet as he refuses their apologies. As Pairo calms him down, the main thug reveals that an old man (the Elder) was the one who hired them to confront them, eventually explaining all the details. Pairo tells him to call the old man and explain that they weren't able to get them angry, which the thug quickly does before continuing to apologize for taking things too far.

As the two prepare to return to the village, a crowd of townspeople gathers behind them, and the old woman from the department store throws small rocks at Kurapika, calling them the devil's messengers and ordering them to leave. Her granddaughter begs her to stop and worries that they will kill them all if they get any angrier. On the return trip, the two discuss what happened, and when Kurapika points out that he failed since his eyes turned scarlet, Pairo reveals that he switched the eyedrops and used his medicine instead. After they return to the village, the Elder tells Kurapika that he has passed and allows him to leave for an indefinite amount of time, also giving him an envelope with the details about Pairo's condition so that he can look for a doctor.

Kurapika prepares to leave and assures Pairo that he will find a doctor for him. However, Pairo reminds him about Dino Hunter and how they wanted to have an adventure, asking him to promise that he will have a trip where he has fun. Kurapika says goodbye to Pairo and his clan, and it's stated that the Kurta Clan massacre was reported to the world six weeks later, an account of which is given in extensive and brutal detail. Lastly, it's mentioned that the following message was found near the 128 bodies: "We'll accept anything you leave, but don't ever take anything away from us."

Trivia

On the inside flap of the physical volume, the art of the main characters is from the cover of the Weekly Shōnen Jump issue in which the first chapter of the one-shot was published.
